macro_markets.query: What are the most important macroeconomic and stock market news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major index movements, Fed/central bank news, and significant company earnings or announcements.

items.title: Major US Stock Indexes Rise on AI Optimism
items.summary: The S&P 500, Dow Jones, and Nasdaq all gained on Tuesday as markets recovered from Monday's AI-driven selloff. The S&P 500 rose 0.8% to 6,890.07, the Dow gained 0.8% to 49,174.50, and the Nasdaq climbed 1% to 22,863.68, recovering nearly three-quarters of the previous day's losses.

items.title: AMD Leads Market Rally on Meta AI Chip Deal
items.summary: AMD helped lead the market after announcing a multiyear deal to supply chips for Meta Platforms' AI infrastructure, signaling continued investment in artificial intelligence technology despite recent market concerns.

items.title: IBM Recovers from Worst Day Since 2000
items.summary: IBM rose 2.7% on Tuesday to recover some of its 13.1% plunge from Monday, which marked its worst trading day since 2000 amid concerns about AI's impact on the software industry.

items.title: Keysight Technologies Surges on Strong Earnings
items.summary: Keysight Technologies rallied 23.1% for the largest S&P 500 gain after beating analyst expectations for profit and revenue, with guidance for roughly 30% revenue growth in the current quarter.

items.title: Home Depot Beats Expectations Despite Consumer Uncertainty
items.summary: Home Depot rose 2% after delivering stronger-than-expected profit and revenue, though CEO Ted Decker noted ongoing consumer uncertainty in the market.

items.title: Consumer Confidence Improves More Than Expected
items.summary: A report showed U.S. consumer confidence improved by more than economists expected, helping stabilize Treasury yields with the 10-year Treasury holding steady at 4.03%.

crypto_news.query: What are the most significant cryptocurrency and blockchain news from the past 24 hours? Include major price movements, regulatory news, and notable project developments.

items.title: ZachXBT to Reveal Major Crypto Investigation on February 26
items.summary: On-chain investigator ZachXBT is set to drop a major investigation on February 26 alleging that multiple employees at one of crypto's most profitable businesses abused internal data for insider trading. Markets are already reacting with prediction platforms heavily skewed toward specific names.

items.title: Bitcoin Drops Below $64,000 as Crypto Market Continues Correction
items.summary: Bitcoin has fallen below $64,000 USDT, trading at $63,996.48, continuing a severe market correction that has erased nearly half of BTC's peak value since October 2025. Bitcoin is on track for its steepest monthly decline since 2022, down approximately 18% in February.

items.title: Ethereum and Altcoins Face Severe Losses Amid $2 Trillion Market Decline
items.summary: Ethereum has dropped 62% from its highs to trade near $1,825, while Solana has fallen 68% and many smaller tokens have lost up to 90% of their value over the past 140 days. Market sentiment has shifted from fear and greed to exhaustion and defeat.

items.title: Crypto.com Receives U.S. Bank Charter Approval
items.summary: Crypto.com has won approval for a U.S. bank charter, marking a significant regulatory milestone for the cryptocurrency exchange industry.

items.title: Bitcoin Options Volatility Reaches Multi-Year Highs Amid Market Stress
items.summary: Bitcoin options implied volatility spiked to multi-year highs on February 5, with 25-delta put IV reaching 95%, reflecting heightened market uncertainty during the acute sell-off phase that saw prices drop from $90,000 to $60,000.

items.title: Institutional Outflows Continue as Crypto Market Sentiment Weakens
items.summary: U.S.-based spot Bitcoin ETFs saw net withdrawals of $1.6 billion in January, while spot Ethereum ETFs experienced $353 million in outflows, signaling reduced institutional participation amid the broader market correction.

ai_news.query: What are the most important artificial intelligence and machine learning news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include new model releases, company announcements, research breakthroughs, and industry trends.

items.title: Fraser Institute Study: AI Could Transform Healthcare with System Redesign
items.summary: A new Fraser Institute study by Avi Goldfarb emphasizes AI's potential for enormous productivity gains in healthcare, including faster diagnosis and shorter wait times, but only if systems are redesigned around AI. It highlights examples like self-driving labs for drug discovery and calls for rethinking workflows, professional boundaries, and regulations.

items.title: US Defense Dept Threatens Anthropic Over AI Restrictions for Military Use
items.summary: The Secretary of Defense is pressuring Anthropic to remove limits on its AI for autonomous weapons and surveillance, threatening to label it a supply chain risk after suspicions of use in a Venezuela attack via Palantir partnership. Anthropic, cleared for classified ops in 2025, faces contract termination if it doesn't comply.

items.title: University of Toronto Researchers Warn AI Removes Essential 'Friction' for Human Growth
items.summary: A new article in Communications Psychology argues that AI's ease in intellectual and social tasks eliminates beneficial friction needed for learning, motivation, and meaningful experiences. Authors Emily Zohar and Michael Inzlicht urge careful integration to preserve human development.

items.title: India AI Summit Pushes Human-Centric AI with Infrastructure and Farmer Tools
items.summary: At the world's largest AI summit, India emphasized human-centric AI under 'Sarvajana Hitaya, Sarvajana Sukhaya,' showcasing tools like AMUL's Sarlaben for 3.6M farmers and Bharat VISTAAR. The India AI Mission deploys GPUs, creates a national AI repository, and builds full value chain infrastructure.

items.title: Study Shows AI Can Predict Active Fund Managers' Trades, Questioning Their Edge
items.summary: New research reveals AI can forecast a significant portion of active fund managers' trading decisions, exposing predictable patterns and raising doubts about their value over passive strategies.

gaming.query: What are the most notable video game and gaming industry news from the past 24 hours? Include game releases, announcements, esports, and industry developments.

items.title: Marvel's Wolverine PS5 Game Release Date Announced for September 2026
items.summary: Insomniac Games' Marvel's Wolverine has been officially scheduled for a September 2026 launch on PlayStation 5. The announcement secures its place in this year's major gaming releases.

items.title: Pokémon Presents Scheduled for February 27, 2026 with 25-Minute Runtime
items.summary: The Pokémon Company will host a Pokémon Day Presents event on February 27 at 2pm UTC, celebrating the series' 30th anniversary. Expectations include updates on remakes like FireRed and LeafGreen, mobile apps, and possible Gen 10 news.

items.title: Take-Two Interactive Stock Drops 9.5% Despite Earnings Beat Amid GTA 6 Delay Fears
items.summary: Take-Two reported strong earnings with NBA 2K growth and mobile momentum, but shares fell due to Bloomberg reporter Jason Schreier's comments on potential GTA 6 delays beyond Spring 2027. Investors remain cautious about the premium-priced title.

items.title: Capcom Shares Surge 10% After Strong Earnings; Resident Evil: Requiem Launches February 27
items.summary: Capcom's stock rose on robust performance, including Street Fighter 6 hitting 6M units and Monster Hunter Wilds sales. Upcoming Resident Evil: Requiem releases on PS5, Xbox, PC, and Switch 2 in three days.

tech_news.query: What are the most important technology news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major product launches, company news, and significant tech industry developments. Exclude AI-specific news.

items.title: Strategy World 2026 Conference Launches in Las Vegas
items.summary: Strategy (formerly MicroStrategy) opened its annual Strategy World 2026 event at The Wynn Las Vegas on February 24, featuring keynotes on enterprise innovation, digital credit via Bitcoin, and over 85 sessions on business intelligence and corporate treasury. The conference runs through February 26 alongside Bitcoin for Corporations.

items.title: Xometry Reports Record Q4 2025 Earnings with New Manufacturing Platform Features
items.summary: Xometry announced record Q4 and full-year 2025 results, launching auto quotes for injection molding in the US and Europe, expanding materials for additive manufacturing, and advancing Teamspace and Thomas platform capabilities. Priorities for 2026 include further injection molding enhancements and e-commerce improvements.

items.title: US Battery Energy Storage Deployments Forecast to Reach 70GWh in 2026
items.summary: SEIA's first sector-dedicated report projects US BESS installations to hit 35GW/70GWh in 2026, up from 28GW/57GWh in 2025, driven by utility-scale growth and $25.2 billion in investments, with 48% paired with solar.

items.title: MarineMax Faces Public Dispute with Donerail Over $1.1 Billion Buyout Offer
items.summary: Donerail Group issued a second open letter urging MarineMax shareholders to push for a $35-per-share acquisition amid concerns over management, while MarineMax responded that its board is evaluating the unsolicited $1.1 billion cash proposal submitted in January.
